name: Divide And Conquer Heuristic Registry
summary: Family-local interpretation of shared heuristics for split, recurse, and merge algorithms.
dejargonized_summary: This registry says which generic warning signs matter most for algorithms that repeatedly split work into subproblems and then merge the results.

admissibility_notes: When most of the work collapses into one branch, the current split strategy is likely too weak.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when repeated decompositions produce highly imbalanced subproblem mass.
family_notes: In divide-and-conquer families, density collapse often appears as split imbalance rather than sample dropout or detector collapse.

admissibility_notes: Unstable recursion depth or merge-cost growth should trigger validation before the family commits to deeper expansion.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when depth or merge cost grows faster than the family expects.
family_notes: This is a family-local reading of a shared resource-growth heuristic.

admissibility_notes: If the split strategy fragments coverage or misses overlap structure, the merge path should not be trusted without validation.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when overlap or partition coverage checks repeatedly fail.
family_notes: Coverage fragmentation is a generic heuristic; this family interprets it in terms of subproblem overlap and partition completeness.

audit.rationale: Proves that family-local heuristic interpretation can stay generic even in a non-signal algorithm family.
audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the generic warning signs that matter most for split-and-merge algorithms and the structural responses they justify.
uncertainty_notes: The registry intentionally interprets shared heuristics locally without redefining them.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.citation: Local transitional family registry for shared heuristic interpretation.
